(Wail? they) MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economics Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, 11th December, '2014 * G.S.R. 888$)r—In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (d) and (e) of sub—section (2) of n. 24 ofthe Coinage Act, 4n 1 14‘ 1 1 of 21:11 1), the Central Govemmeni hereby makes the following rules, I 1. Short title and commencement—(1) These rules may be called the Coinage of Fifty Rupees and 'Five Rupees - Coins to mark the occasion of BHEL - 50 YEARS OF ENGINEERIN EXCELLENCE Rules, 2014. . (2) They shall come into force on the date Of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Denomination of coins—The coins of the following denominations shall also be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of “BHEL - 50 YEARS OF ENGINEERING EXCELLENCE”, namely:— (i) Fifiy Rupees; (ii) Five Rupees. 3. Dimension and composition of coins.———The coins of the denominations mentioned in rule 2 shall ' ' conform to the following dimensions and composition, namely:— Denomination - Shape and outside ‘ - Number of . . -. . . - Metal composmon of com diameter serratlons ‘(1) (2) ' . (3) p (4) ‘ y Quaternary Alloy , - Circular . Silver-50 per cent. Fifiy Rupees 39 millimeters 180 Copper-40 per cent. ' ‘ Nickel—05 per cent. Zinc—05 per cent. . . Nickel Brass . Circular Co er-75 er cent Five Rupees 23 millimeters 100 Z. W p ' . ~ ~1nc-20 per cent. Nickel-05 per cent. 4. Design—The design of the coins shall be as under:— (i) FIFTY RUPEES ' " OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “W m’ inscribed below, flanked on the left upper periphery with the word “W’ in Devnagri script and on the right upper periphery flanked, with the word “INDIA” in English, and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol “?” and denominational value “50” in the International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. -- 3 of 4 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] REVERSE: . , . The face of the coin shall bear the logo of “GOLDEN JUBILEE 0F BHEL” in the centre, with inscription “WW WE?” a? 50 ml” in Devnagri script on upper periphery and the lower periphery shall bear the inscription “50 YEARS OF ENGINEERING EXCELLENCE” in English, and the year’s figure “1964-2014” shall be written below the logo1n intematiOnal numerals. (i) FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar1n the centre with the legend “W W’ inscribed below, flanked on the left upper periphery with the word “HR?!” in Devnagri script and '1 on the right upper periphery flanked with the word “INDIA” in English, and it shall also bear the Rupee , symbol “?” and denominational value “5” in the international numerals below the Lion Capitol. w The face of the coin shall bear the logo of “GOLDEN JUBILEE OF BHEL” in the centre, with inscription “W WET 31' 50 W” in Devnagri script on upper periphery and the lower periphery shall bear the inscription “59 YEARS nF ENGINEEPWC EXCELLENCEs in English, and the year’s figure “1964-2014” shall be written below the logo1n international numerals. (ii) Standard weight and remedy allowed.- The standard weight of the coins of the following denominations, coined to commemorate the occasion of “BHEL — 50 YEARS OF ENGINEERING EXCELLENCE” and the remedy allowed'1n making of such coins shall be as specified1n the Table below:—— TABLE Denomination . Remedy allowed . of coin Standard we1ght In composition . In standard weight (1) (2) .
